title: A pet or an animal I see often
summary: Describe a pet or an animal you see often and what you do together.
topic: A pet or an animal I see often
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a pet or an animal you see often. Describe the animal and what you do together. Use present simple.
hintStarters: There is a cat / dog / parrot that…
I see this animal every day when…
sampleText: There is a tabby cat in the courtyard of my building in Lisbon. His name is Ruivo, which means 'ginger' in Portuguese. He has orange and white fur and long whiskers. I see him every morning when I go outside. He always runs to me and rubs his head on my leg. I give him a small piece of bread or sometimes a bit of fish. He eats quickly and then he sits next to me. I stroke his fur and he makes a soft purring sound. Ruivo is friendly with everyone in the building. The old lady upstairs also feeds him every evening. He is not my cat, but he feels like a neighbour. He sleeps under the stairs when it is cold. I always feel calm when I sit with him in the sunny courtyard. He is a small animal, but he makes the mornings better.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: rubs his head on my leg

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for descriptions and habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để mô tả đặc điểm và thói quen: 'He has orange fur', 'I see him every morning', 'He always runs to me'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to describe characteristics and habits, as in 'He has orange fur' and 'I see him every morning'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always' đặt trước động từ chính: 'He always runs to me', 'I always feel calm'. Khi động từ là 'be', đặt adverb sau: 'He is not my cat'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always' goes before the main verb: 'He always runs to me'. With the verb 'be', place the adverb after it: 'He is always friendly'.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một con mèo kẻ sọc trong sân trong của tòa nhà tôi ở Lisbon. Tên nó là Ruivo, có nghĩa là 'gừng' trong tiếng Bồ Đào Nha. Nó có bộ lông cam và trắng và bộ râu dài. Tôi thấy nó mỗi sáng khi ra ngoài. Nó luôn chạy đến và dụi đầu vào chân tôi. Tôi cho nó một miếng bánh mì nhỏ hoặc đôi khi một ít cá. Nó ăn nhanh rồi ngồi cạnh tôi. Tôi vuốt lông và nó phát ra tiếng gừ gừ nhẹ nhàng. Ruivo thân thiện với tất cả mọi người trong tòa nhà. Bà cụ tầng trên cũng cho nó ăn mỗi buổi tối. Nó không phải mèo của tôi, nhưng cảm giác như hàng xóm. Nó ngủ dưới cầu thang khi trời lạnh. Tôi luôn cảm thấy bình yên khi ngồi với nó trong sân đầy nắng. Nó là một con vật nhỏ, nhưng khiến buổi sáng trở nên tốt hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes an animal's appearance?
options: He having orange and white fur.
He has orange and white fur.
He have orange and white fur.
He is have orange and white fur.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Has' is the correct third-person singular form of 'have' in the present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which adverb fits: 'He ___ runs to me when I go outside.'
mcQuestions.explanation: The context shows a regular, expected habit — 'always' is the best fit.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose the adverb that shows this happens every time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple correctly?
options: The cat is sleeping every morning under the stairs.
The cat sleeps under the stairs when it is cold.
The cat sleeping under the stairs.
The cat slept under the stairs every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'sleeps' is correct for a regular habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the simple form without -ing or -ed.

mcQuestions.explanation: 'Purr' is the quiet, vibrating sound a cat makes when it is happy.
mcQuestions.hint: Think of the sound a happy cat makes.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes what someone does with an animal?
options: I stroke his fur and he purrs.
I stroke his fur and he is purring always.
I stroking his fur and he purr.
I stroked his fur every morning yesterday.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs are in the present simple, which is correct for a regular activity.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in the present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which preposition fits: 'He sleeps ___ the stairs.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Under' means below a surface; it fits a cat sheltering beneath stairs.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about a lower position, below something.

options: I see him every of the mornings.
I see him each every morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every' is followed by a singular noun: 'every morning'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence gives a simple description of an animal's character?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'He is friendly with everyone' uses the verb 'be' and the correct preposition 'with'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker fits best: 'He is not my cat, ___ he feels like a neighbour.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows a contrast between the two ideas.
mcQuestions.hint: Choose the linker that shows an unexpected difference.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence fits the topic 'an animal I see often' best?
options: I once saw a lion at the zoo.
I see a tabby cat in the courtyard every morning.
My favourite animal is an elephant.
mcQuestions.explanation: It uses 'see', a frequency phrase, and a specific location — fitting the topic exactly.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a regular action with a specific animal and place.

criticalThinkingQuestion: How can animals like stray cats or street dogs become part of a neighbourhood community?
